rationale: Review 1 correctly identifies, from the diff, that `BialgHom.map_antipode`, `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map_id`, and `map_comp` are general results marked `private`, making them invisible to downstream TauCeti files — a real reuse defect. It also correctly calls out `includeRight_apply` as a `rfl` no-op. Review 2 misses both issues entirely. Some of Review 1's other findings (removing `map_tmul`, `map_toAlgHom`) are contested and could hurt the API, but the private-helper finding alone is material enough to prefer the first review.
